title: AgenticPeak Agent Specification
description: Canonical machine-readable spec for an agent. Read by the Agent Factory to generate main.py and deploy to AgentCore.

spec_version.description: Schema version. Pinned to 1.0 — bump only when breaking schema changes.

agent_id.description: Spec identifier — kebab-case, used for filename. Example: agent-01-free-website-audit. Distinct from agent_name (runtime name).

agent_name.description: AgentCore runtime name — camelCase, no spaces/hyphens. Example: freeWebsiteAudit, pipelineMonitor.

tenant_id.description: Tenant identifier. SEO Local is tenant-001. Future tenants: tenant-002, etc.

phase.description: Which build phase this agent belongs to.

description.description: Plain-English description of what the agent does. Used as AgentCore runtime description.

type.description: Primary trigger mechanism. http_webhook = called via HTTP bridge. cron = EventBridge schedule. event_bus = SQS message from agenticpeak-agents bus. manual = invoked directly.

schedule.description: EventBridge cron expression. Required if type=cron. Example: 'rate(4 hours)' or 'cron(0 */4 * * ? *)'.

event_pattern.description: EventBridge event pattern for filtering. Required if type=event_bus.

webhook_source.description: Source system that calls this webhook. Optional documentation. Example: 'GHL workflow', 'Recurly webhook'.

path.description: URL path. Must start with /. Examples: /audit/website, /amplify/testimonial.

handler.description: Python function name in main.py that handles this path. Example: handle_audit. Generator creates this function.

validation.description: Optional validation rule. Example: 'http(s):// URL', 'GHL contact_id format'.

ssm_params.description: SSM Parameter Store params this agent reads at runtime. Factory validates these exist before deploy.

required.description: Required SSM params. Agent fails to start if missing.

optional.description: Optional SSM params. Agent runs but with degraded functionality if missing.

apis.description: External APIs this agent uses. Generator imports the right SDK + builds adapter calls.

adapters.description: Abstract adapter usage. Set true if agent uses the adapter; the factory injects the lib code.

crm.description: Use CRMAdapter (GHL concrete). Factory imports lib/adapters/crm_adapter.py.

billing.description: Use BillingAdapter (Recurly concrete). Factory imports lib/adapters/billing_adapter.py.

memory.description: AgentCore Memory configuration. Replaces RDS for state.

strategy.description: Memory strategy. episodic = action history. semantic = extracted facts. both = full memory.

namespace_key.description: Input field name whose runtime value scopes the memory namespace. Example: 'lead_id' produces namespace 'tenant-001:lead-abc123'. Memory is per-entity isolated.

target_agent_id.description: agent_id of the agent being invoked. Factory looks up runtime ARN from SSM key VIDEO_ENGINE_RUNTIME_ARN, etc.

trigger_condition.description: Plain-English condition that triggers chain-invoke. Example: 'on milestone detected', 'on testimonial scored above 15'.

ssm_arn_key.description: SSM key holding the target agent's runtime ARN. Example: VIDEO_ENGINE_RUNTIME_ARN.

chain_invokes.description: Other agents this agent calls. Factory generates the boto3 invoke code.

extra_packages.description: Extra pip packages beyond the standard set (bedrock-agentcore, boto3, requests). Example: ['anthropic', 'reportlab', 'psycopg2-binary'].

llm_tasks.description: List of LLM tasks this agent performs. Per Chuck's LLM-SELECTOR-SPEC.md (2026-04-27). Each task maps to a complexity tier: COMPLEX=Sonnet, STANDARD=Haiku (default), SIMPLE=Nova Micro. The factory's code generator emits one invoke_llm() call per task, with the correct complexity baked in. Empty array means the agent makes no LLM calls (e.g., pure rules-based agents like rank_check or lce_data).

task_name.description: Internal name for this task. Used as the variable name and code comment in generated main.py. Example: qualify_lead, generate_followup_message, score_testimonial.

complexity.description: Maps to a model tier per LLM-SELECTOR-SPEC.md. COMPLEX=claude-sonnet-4-6 (multi-step reasoning, content generation, strategy). STANDARD=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (classification, extraction, routing — DEFAULT). SIMPLE=amazon.nova-micro-v1:0 (lookups, formatting, yes/no). Never default to COMPLEX.

description.description: What this task does. Used as documentation and code comment in generated main.py.

system_prompt_hint.description: Optional. A one-line description of the system prompt role. Generator emits this as the system_prompt parameter. Example: 'You are a lead qualification specialist'.
